summary:
Claim: The middle finger is the only finger with two dorsal interossei.  
Evidence: - Central location enables balanced mechanical leverage and symmetric muscle engagement. - Median nerve innervation supports functional necessity for dual interosseous attachment in this digit.  
Answer: C
